chapter_title.ar: باب الْبِئْرِ وَ مَا يَقَعُ فِيهَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 4 – The water well and what falls into it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/26)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/26)
text: لَا يُفْسِدُ الْمَاءَ إِلَّا مَا كَانَ لَهُ نَفْسٌ سَائِلَةٌ .

en.hubeali: (It has been narrated) raising it from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘It would not pollute the water except if it has a spurting blood for it (warm blooded mammal)’.
en.sarwar: 4. Muhammad ibn Yahya in a marfu‘ manner has narrated the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Water does not become polluted because of the living things which do not have spurting blood when a cut is made in its body.’”
